close_reason: Already documented in struct-level docs with explicit workaround (reconstruct backend with fresh token). Token refresh is deferred to a future enhancement.
description: In secretx-gcp-sm/src/lib.rs, the GCP access token is fetched from the environment at GcpSmBackend::from_uri construction time and reused for the lifetime of the backend. GCP access tokens expire after 1 hour. After expiry, get()/refresh() calls return SecretError::Unavailable. The limitation is documented in the module-level docs and the struct doc, with a workaround (reconstruct the backend, or use Workload Identity). However, the suggested workaround of reconstructing on Unavailable is ambiguous: Unavailable is also returned for real transient errors (network down, 5xx), so callers cannot distinguish 'token expired' from 'server down'. The 401 case should be mapped to a distinct variant (e.g. SecretError::CredentialsExpired or a specific Backend error), or the Unavailable message should be stable enough to allow callers to detect it. Currently, distinguishing token expiry from true transient unavailability requires parsing the error source string.
title: gcp-sm: access token never refreshed after construction
